SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.core.store

Class DefaultFailoverStore<T>

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.core.store.DefaultFailoverStore<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of the payload held by each referential entry
All Implemented Interfaces:
FailoverStore<T>
public class DefaultFailoverStore<T> extends Object implements FailoverStore<T>
Default FailoverStore decorator that ensures every payload written to or read from the delegate store has its upToDate flag forced to false.

This prevents stale failover data from being mistaken for a fresh response: consumers can check upToDate to distinguish a live result from a failover fallback. The cleanByExpiry(java.time.Instant) operation is delegated as-is since it does not produce payloads.

    Description
    void
    cleanByExpiry(Instant expiry)
    Delegates expiry-based cleanup directly to the underlying store without modification.

    Deletes a copy of the given payload with upToDate forced to false, then delegates to the underlying store.

    find(String name, String key)
    Looks up a payload by name and key, returning a copy with upToDate forced to false if found.
    List<ReferentialPayload<T>>
    findAll(String name)
    Returns all payloads for the given name, each as a defensive copy with upToDate forced to false.

    Stores a copy of the given payload with upToDate forced to false, then delegates to the underlying store.

      public DefaultFailoverStore()

  • Method Details

    • store

      public void store(ReferentialPayload<T> referentialPayload) throws FailoverStoreException
      Stores a copy of the given payload with upToDate forced to false, then delegates to the underlying store.
      Specified by:
      store in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      referentialPayload - the payload to persist; must not be null
      Throws:
      FailoverStoreException - if the delegate store operation fails

    • delete

      public void delete(ReferentialPayload<T> referentialPayload) throws FailoverStoreException
      Deletes a copy of the given payload with upToDate forced to false, then delegates to the underlying store.
      Specified by:
      delete in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      referentialPayload - the payload to remove; must not be null
      Throws:
      FailoverStoreException - if the delegate delete operation fails

    • find

      public Optional<ReferentialPayload<T>> find(String name, String key) throws FailoverStoreException
      Looks up a payload by name and key, returning a copy with upToDate forced to false if found.
      Specified by:
      find in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      name - the referential name
      key - the unique key within that referential
      Returns:
      an Optional containing a defensive copy of the stored payload with upToDate=false, or empty if not found
      Throws:
      FailoverStoreException - if the delegate lookup operation fails

    • findAll

      public List<ReferentialPayload<T>> findAll(String name) throws FailoverStoreException
      Returns all payloads for the given name, each as a defensive copy with upToDate forced to false.

      This is the enforcement point for the FailoverStore.findAll(java.lang.String) defensive-copy contract: standard stores delegating through DefaultFailoverStore satisfy it automatically.

      Specified by:
      findAll in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      name - the referential name
      Returns:
      defensive copies of all matching payloads with upToDate=false, or an empty list
      Throws:
      FailoverStoreException - if the delegate lookup operation fails

    • cleanByExpiry

      public void cleanByExpiry(Instant expiry) throws FailoverStoreException
      Delegates expiry-based cleanup directly to the underlying store without modification.
      Specified by:
      cleanByExpiry in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      expiry - the cutoff instant; entries whose expireOn is before this value are removed
      Throws:
      FailoverStoreException - if the delegate cleanup operation fails
